blocks.text: Cindergrove Advisors Inc
Mr. Darryl Rogers, VP Finance, Snow-Berry
Re: Inventory Control Study
Dear Mr. Rogers:

This letter will confirm the arrangements we have discussed for an inventory control study at {{fact:f:E-2001-001.client}}. We appreciate the confidence Snow-Berry has placed in our firm, and we are prepared to begin work on {{fact:f:E-2001-001.start}}.

The purpose of the study is to examine the methods by which Snow-Berry orders, receives, stores, and issues inventory, and to recommend improvements in the accuracy of the records and the control of stock. Our work will proceed in the manner described in our proposal: interviews with the responsible personnel, observation of operations at the warehouse and on the plant floor, analysis of the ordering and count records, and a written report setting out our findings together with a practical program for carrying out the recommendations. We expect the study to run approximately five months from the start of fieldwork.

Kimberly Price, Senior Consultant, will direct the engagement and will be your principal contact for its day-to-day conduct. Andrea Diaz, Consultant, will perform the on-site fieldwork and the review of records. I will review all work personally before it leaves our office.

Our fee for the study will be {{fact:f:E-2001-001.fee}}, which covers professional time and ordinary out-of-pocket expenses. Should the scope of the work change materially, we will advise you in writing before proceeding, and no addition to the fee will be made without your written approval.

If these terms are satisfactory, please sign both copies of this letter where indicated, return one to our office by fax, and retain the other for your file. The countersigned original may follow by mail. We are pleased to be of service to {{fact:f:E-2001-001.client}} and look forward to a productive engagement.
Very truly yours,
Memorandum
To: Engagement Team, Snow-Berry Inventory Control Study
From: Kimberly Price, Senior Consultant
Re: Kickoff of the Snow-Berry engagement

Fieldwork on our engagement for {{fact:f:E-2001-001.client}} began on {{fact:f:E-2001-001.start}}. The objective of the study is straightforward: determine why the inventory records do not agree with what is on the shelf, and leave the client with a program its own people can run to keep the records honest. Darryl Rogers, VP Finance, is our client contact; all requests for records and plant access go through his office.
Workstreams

items: Receiving and put-away: observe current practice at the dock and in the warehouse, and document the paperwork that follows the material.
Record accuracy: compare the perpetual records against physical counts and trace the sources of the differences.
Ordering and replenishment: review how reorder points and order quantities are set and who authorizes purchases.
Reporting: define the plain-numbers reports management should see each month.

blocks.text: Next Steps

Andrea Diaz will be on site through the first phase of fieldwork and will keep the working file. I will schedule a working session with Darryl Rogers once the first round of observations is complete. Please direct questions to me. Nothing goes to the client in writing without my review.
Meeting Minutes: Inventory Control Study Working Session
Working session held at the offices of {{fact:f:E-2001-001.client}} to review progress on the inventory control study. Minutes prepared by Andrea Diaz.
Attendees

items: Kimberly Price, Senior Consultant, Cindergrove Advisors Inc
Andrea Diaz, Consultant, Cindergrove Advisors Inc

blocks.text: Discussion

Kimberly Price opened the session with a summary of the fieldwork completed to date. Observation of receiving and put-away practice is finished, and the comparison of the perpetual records against physical counts is under way. She noted that the differences observed so far follow a pattern consistent with paperwork lagging the movement of material rather than with loss of stock, though she cautioned that this reading is preliminary until the record tracing is complete.

Darryl Rogers reported that the warehouse staff have been cooperative and asked whether the study would take up the setting of order quantities. Kimberly Price confirmed that ordering practice is within scope for the next phase. Andrea Diaz raised one open item: the adjustment records from the prior physical inventory have not yet been located, and the tracing work cannot be finished without them.
Action Items

rows: Locate the adjustment records from the prior physical inventory and provide them to the consulting team
Complete the tracing of record differences and summarize the findings for the next session
Prepare the outline of the interim report for internal review
